claim: Slot 07 (Okamoto 1987 FE 30 P_III) opens at SHA-256 65294FBCA97E3CE1DB0EA193DCD3048D9A2942DB2AE6D435C9BDFACC2B78A43F (file size 6075525 bytes, 28 pages, pypdf-readable text 56170 chars).

Okamoto 1987 sec.0 (p.306) labels the P_III' Hamiltonian as H_III' = (1/t)[q^2 p^2 - { eta_inf q^2 + theta_0 q - eta_0 t } p + (1/2) eta_inf (theta_0 + theta_inf) q], and immediately sets eta_Delta = 1 WLOG, leaving (theta_0, theta_inf) as the parameter pair.
Okamoto 1987 eq. (0.5) (p.307) defines the parameter base space as 2-dimensional: V is 2-dim complex with v_1 = theta_0, v_2 = theta_inf. The Painleve system P(v) is fibred over V.

Okamoto 1987 sec.2.1 (p.317-318) defines the root system R = R_III' of type B_2 on V with fundamental roots alpha_1 = e_1 - e_2, alpha_2 = e_2, highest root alpha = e_1 + e_2; reflections s_1: (v_1,v_2) -> (v_2,v_1), s_2: (v_1,v_2) -> (v_1,-v_2), s_0 about affine wall (alpha|v) = -1, i.e. v_1 + v_2 = -1.

Okamoto 1987 sec.2 contains no '4-tuple sum' canonical relation R1; the canonical relations are encoded as W(B_2) action on the 2-dim V, with reflection walls v_1 - v_2 = 0 (alpha_1), v_2 = 0 (alpha_2), v_1 + v_2 = -1 (alpha affine). A Bessel-class sub-locus appears at v_1 + v_2 = 0 (Okamoto p.308).
Phase C arithmetic: alpha_inf + alpha_0 + beta_inf + beta_0 = 1/6 + 0 + 0 + (-1/2) = -1/3, NOT 0. The brief-stated sum-to-zero constraint is therefore not satisfied.

Phase C interpretation: the CT v1.3 sec.3.5 4-tuple labeling (alpha_inf, alpha_0, beta_inf, beta_0) does not exist in Okamoto 1987 (which uses a 2-parameter (theta_0, theta_inf) convention). G18 is therefore a labeling-correspondence artifact (interpretation (a) per prompt 020 sec.0), not a substantive constraint failure.

Cross-check (Phase C.4): Barhoumi-Lisovyy-Miller-Prokhorov 2024 (slot 08) likewise treats P_III(D_6) as a 2-parameter family (alpha, beta) with monodromy parameters (e_1, e_2). It does not impose a 4-tuple linear sum constraint either; the 4-tuple labeling in CT v1.3 sec.3.5 is thus a separate (likely Jimbo-Miwa Fuchs-relation or Sakai surface) convention not aligned with either primary source acquired so far.

Verdict: UPGRADE_G18_CLOSED_PARAMETER_CORRESPONDENCE_OKAMOTO_2PARAM_VS_CT_4TUPLE. Okamoto 1987 sec.2 R1 is the W(B_2) root-system action on the 2-dim parameter space V; no 4-tuple sum constraint is imposed. The CT v1.3 sec.3.5 numerical mismatch arises from labeling, not from a constraint violation.
